original_abstract: Practical activities remain one of the most effective methodological tools for enhancing the teaching-learning process. Beyond simply sparking an interest in science, these hands-on experiences foster the construction of knowledge and encourage students to develop a critical perspective on the world around them. Despite these benefits, traditional lectures continue to dominate the educational landscape. This study evaluated, characterized, and promoted the use of practical science education within two schools in Vitória de Santo Antão, PE: one rural (Duque de Caxias) and one urban (August 3). Data were collected through site visits and teacher questionnaires. The findings indicate that the Duque de Caxias school possesses better infrastructure and demonstrates a stronger faculty commitment to practical instruction compared to the August 3 school. Both institutions identified a lack of resources, overcrowded classrooms, and limited teacher availability as primary barriers to implementing experimental activities. To address these challenges, specialized laboratory manuals were developed for the 6th through 9th grades to better support both educators and students in the classroom.
humanized_abstract: For many years, the practice activities have been an important pedagogical tool for enhancing the teaching and learning process. In addition to stimulating the interest of students for the area of science, the practical activities enable the construction of knowledge and contribute to the development of a critical view of the world. However, the lectures remain the most used educational strategy. This study aimed to evaluate, recognize and strengthen the practical science teaching in two schools located in Vitória de Santo Antão, PE: a rural school, Duque de Caxias, and an urban school, August 3. The information was collected in visits to the schools and through the application of questionnaires with teachers. The Duque de Caxias school has better infrastructure and a greater involvement of teachers in the realization of practical classes than the August 3 school. Both schools have listed several obstacles to the realization of experimental classes in the classrooms, such as lack of materials, large number of students and lack of time for the preparation of classes. To contribute to the strengthening of the practical activities in the schools, laboratory manuals specialized for the 6th to 9th grades were developed.

This study aimed to evaluate, recognize and strengthen the practical science teaching in two schools located in Vitória de Santo Antão, PE: a rural school, Duque de Caxias, and an urban school, August 3. The Duque de Caxias school has better infrastructure and a greater involvement of teachers in the realization of practical classes than the August 3 school. Both schools have listed several obstacles to the realization of experimental classes in the classrooms, such as lack of materials, large number of students and lack of time for the preparation of classes. To contribute to the strengthening of the practical activities in the schools, laboratory manuals specialized for the 6th to 9th grades were developed.
